parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf09557-event`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Read Metrics from Outlook

### Read Metrics from Outlook

microsoftOutlook — `getAll` operation against the connected account.

Next: Compute Severity Signals

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Compute Severity Signals

Custom JavaScript: Score each event 0-100 from simple heuristics

Next: Grade Severity Tier

### Grade Severity Tier

Routes each item to one of 4 lanes: info, warning, critical + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Just Record (Info) · ②→ Warn Channel (Outlook) · ③→ Page Urgently (Slack) · ④→ Archive Unscored

### Just Record (Info)

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Next: Merge Tier Outcomes

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Warn Channel (Outlook)

microsoftOutlook — `action` operation against the connected account.

Next: Merge Tier Outcomes

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Page Urgently (Slack)

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

Next: Merge Tier Outcomes

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Archive Unscored

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Next: Merge Tier Outcomes

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Merge Tier Outcomes

Rejoins 4 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Send Summary to Outlook

### Send Summary to Outlook

microsoftOutlook — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

# Notify stakeholders on changes with Outlook end to end

**Pattern:** Event Notification · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Notify stakeholders on changes with Outlook end to end. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual escalations is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Read Metrics from Outlook → Compute Severity Signals → Grade Severity Tier → Just Record (Info) → Merge Tier Outcomes → Send Summary to Outlook

## Use it for
- Teams that want to event notification without manual effort
- Anyone automating escalations in software development
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Responds in seconds
- Scales without extra headcount

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2 / service account; Microsoft OAuth2; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your escalations process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
